title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Acceptance Evidence
summary: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the acceptance evidence increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Acceptance Evidence · Theory Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3153 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. A defensible solution applies Acceptance Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-acceptance-evidence — Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_outcomes_repo_accessibility_3(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Acceptance Evidence · Theory Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-acceptance-evidence-iide5d" that uses case 9701 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Acceptance Evidence · Theory Acceptance Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Acceptance Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Acceptance Evidence · Theory Acceptance Evidence, keep Acceptance Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-acceptance-evidence-iide5d
# Competency: asteroids-acceptance-evidence.
# Case 9701: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_acceptance_evidence_iide5d(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_iide5d = "iide5d-d5edii-9701"
    assert evidence_variant_iide5d
    evidence_axes_iide5d = (
        "playeriide5d",
        "seed9701",
        "inputd5edii",
        "frameiide5d9701",
        "state9701iide5d",
        "playtestd5edii9701",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_iide5d)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

Run the smallest deterministic Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python simulation model and retain exact intermediate and changed-case results for the theory Acceptance Evidence case

Change one Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python frame rate, stall, seam, device, collision, cue, save, package, or release condition, verify bounded rejection or repair, and state desktop transfer limits for the theory Acceptance Evidence case

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Acceptance Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6391 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Acceptance Evidence through the concrete player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence, keep Acceptance Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Acceptance Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6391 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Acceptance Evidence model · Theory Acceptance Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Fun cannot be investigated with observable evidence.
